today_in_history_posts.title: Making the case for flag day activation of taproot
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The bitcoin community debates taproot activation, risking missing its benefits without consensus.
- Flag day activation excludes miners, opting for a block height to enforce new rules.
- This method addresses objections, reduces risks, and relies on community and user support for decisions.

today_in_history_posts.title: Increase channel-jamming capital requirements by not counting dust HTLCs
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Eugene proposes increasing capital needs to counter channel-jamming by adjusting HTLC policies.
- His suggestion involves trimming dust HTLC outputs to preserve non-dust HTLC throughput.
- He invites feedback on creating a limit for dust HTLCs without affecting overall channel capacity.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin scaling involves creating 50,000 off-chain "bitcoin banks" for large user transactions.
- These entities range from federated sidechains to time-bound smart contract coinpools, enhancing liquidity and security.
- Addressing scalability with hybrid solutions and secure layer protocols is essential, focusing on auditability and regulatory compliance.
